symptoms.symptom_name: Low-impact mechanical fall from standing height

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: In an osteoporotic elder a standing-height fall is the classic fragility-fracture mechanism.
Same mechanism can fracture the pelvic ring.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe deep groin and hip pain, worst on movement

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Groin/hip pain localised and movement-provoked is typical of proximal femoral fracture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Complete inability to bear weight on the affected limb

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inability to weight-bear after a fall is a cardinal red flag for significant fracture — mandates imaging.
A simple contusion rarely abolishes weight-bearing entirely.
The classic shortened/externally rotated limb is the hallmark deformity of a displaced NOF fracture. SEALED — requires clinician inspection; withheld from the AI-Doctor-readable layer.
Posterior dislocation classically shows internal rotation, not external.

symptoms.symptom_name: Greater-trochanter bony tenderness and pain on passive internal rotation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Focal bony tenderness and exquisite pain on rotation support a proximal femoral fracture — clinician-elicited, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact distal pulses and foot sensation (no gross neurovascular compromise)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring pertinent finding that does not change the need for emergency transport — clinician-examined, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cortical disruption on plain hip radiograph

investigation_details.investigation_name: Plain radiograph of hip / pelvis (AP and lateral)
investigation_details.expected_result: Cortical disruption of the femoral neck confirming fracture (MRI if occult and clinical suspicion remains)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Fracture remains unconfirmed and surgical planning is delayed; a non-weight-bearing faller cannot be cleared clinically.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Radiographic cortical disruption is the confirmatory test — hospital investigation, never available via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known severe osteoporosis with previous fragility (Colles) fracture

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Established osteoporosis and a prior fragility fracture markedly raise the pre-test probability of a new fragility fracture.
May reflect pain and early hypovolaemia from occult thigh haemorrhage. The pale/sweaty appearance is carer-observable (DI-005); the measured heart rate is a clinician/device value and is sealed.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Plain radiograph of hip and pelvis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the fracture and guides surgical planning; performed on ED arrival, not obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods (FBE, U&E/creatinine, group and hold, coagulation)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess for anaemia from occult blood loss, renal function and long-lie complications (rhabdomyolysis/AKI); pre-operative workup — hospital-based.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Remote attempt to obtain a home BP or examine the leg by the AI Doctor
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The AI cannot measure vitals without a device or inspect/palpate the limb; time must not be spent on this instead of calling 000.
